title: Shortcut conflict with national keyboard mapping
author: mfigatowski
body_snippet:
Hi,
I'm using mRemoteNG with polish keyboard settings. I can't type two polish national characters (ć - Control-Alt-C, ś - Control-Alt-S) in  web panels (HTTP/HTTPS protocol).

## Expected Behavior
National characters correctly passed to HTTP/HTTPS connections

## Current Behavior
Control-Alt-C switches from web page text box to Connection / Config panel

## Possible Solution
A way to redefine shortcuts in mRemoteNG. Or to disable it.

## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
1. Set keyboar...

iterations.description: Keyboard shortcuts conflict with national keyboard mappings, impacting usability for users with non-English layouts.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `38757933` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Shortcut conflict with national keyboard mapping

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Keyboard shortcuts conflict with national keyboard mappings, impacting usability for users with non-English layouts.
Approach: Investigate `ProcessCmdKey` and `ShortcutKeys` implementations to ensure correct interpretation of key presses across different national keyboard layouts, potentially leveraging virtual key codes or Windows API for input language handling.
